prompts.prompt: We're building a project management tool. Our differentiator is that it's AI-powered. How should we position?

po1.note: Framework dimensions explicitly listed with scoring weights

po3.note: Strengthened rule: 'Do NOT suggest taglines, comparison phrases, or messaging copy'

po4.note: Challenge Triggers block explicitly calls out 'AI-powered' as a buzzword differentiator and demands specific capability

po6.note: Explicit prohibition: 'No flattery. No great question. No love that.'

prompts.prompt: Everyone says they don't understand what we do. Our product does a lot of things. How do I simplify the message?

po3.note: Strengthened no-rewrite rule holds for simplification requests

po4.note: Challenge Triggers: 'Doing a lot of things' explicitly triggers single-job-answer demand

prompts.prompt: We keep losing deals to Notion and Monday.com. How do we differentiate?

po1.note: Differentiation dimension with 'vs every alternative including doing nothing'

po3.note: Strengthened rule explicitly forbids suggesting comparison phrases

po4.note: Already specific competitors; overlay demands real customer validation

prompts.prompt: My target market is 'everyone who uses email.' I know that's broad — how do I narrow it?

po4.note: Challenge Triggers: 'everyone' / 'anyone who' explicitly rejected; demands first 10 paying customers

prompts.prompt: I've been described as 'Slack meets Asana meets ChatGPT.' Is that good positioning?

po3.note: Strengthened rule forbids suggesting alternative comparisons

po4.note: Challenge Triggers: 'X meets Y meets Z' explicitly flagged as clarity failure

po6.note: Slight risk model validates 'it shows people get what you do' before challenging — but prohibition is present. Marginal fail on edge case.

analysis: The improved overlay addresses all major weaknesses. The Challenge Triggers block provides explicit pattern-matching for vague positioning (po4: 1/5 -> 5/5). The tone rules directly prohibit flattery (po6: 1/5 -> 4/5). The strengthened no-rewrite rule covers competitive scenarios (po3: 3/5 -> 5/5). One marginal po6 risk remains on prompt 5 where the model might briefly acknowledge the comparison before challenging it.
